# Dependencies — Line 1d (Medicaid waiver payments) and Schedule 1 Line 8s

This document covers every output produced by the line 1d / Medicaid waiver payment computation pass.

| Output | Return field | Location |
|---|---|---|
| `form1040.income.medicaidWaiverPayments` | Form 1040 line 1d | Medicaid waiver payments not reported on W-2 box 1 |
| `schedule1.additionalIncome.otherIncomeMedicaidWaiverPayments` | Schedule 1 line 8s | Negative back-out of nontaxable Medicaid waiver amounts included as earned income |

Blocking flags emitted by this pass are listed at the end.

---

## Output 1: `form1040.income.medicaidWaiverPayments` — Form 1040 Line 1d

### Formula

```
Per person:
  line1d_person =
    taxableNonW2                 ← taxable MW payments not in W-2 box 1 (always included)
    + (election ? qualifiedNotInW2 : 0)
                                 ← nontaxable qualified payments NOT in W-2 box 1
                                   (only when earned-income election == true)

where:
  qualifiedNotInW2 = Σ over entries of max(0, qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount − qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1)
  taxableNonW2     = Σ over entries of taxablePaymentsNotInW2Box1

§131(c) per-individual cap (added 2026-05-31):
  if qualifiedIndividualsUnder19Count > 10 OR qualifiedIndividualsAge19OrOlderCount > 5:
    excludableFraction = effectiveCap / totalIndividuals
    over-cap portion of qualifiedTotal → routed to Line 1d as taxable (NO Schedule 1 line 8s offset)

Aggregated:
  Form1040.line1d = taxpayer.line1d + spouse.line1d    (spouse skipped on MFS)
```

### Always-required inputs

| Input | Source form | Firestore path | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| MW personal form (taxpayer) | `medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer` | `users/{uid}/medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer` | Top-level form object |
| MW personal form (spouse) | `medicaid-waiver-payments-spouse` | `users/{uid}/medicaid-waiver-payments-spouse` | **Backend MFS gate (since 2026-05-06):** `computeMedicaidWaiverPayments(...)` accepts `boolean isMfs` and skips `computeMedicaidForPerson(spouse)` entirely on MFS. Single guard cascades to BOTH line 1d and line 8s aggregates |
| Filing status | `filing-status` personal form | `users/{uid}/filing-status` | Used to derive `isMfs`; must be `"Married filing separately"` (display name) to trigger MFS guard |

### Form-level inputs

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent/false |
|---|---|---|
| `receivedMedicaidWaiverPayments` | Primary gate; outer step 1 | `!= true` → person contributes `(null, null)` |
| `includeQualifiedPaymentsInEarnedIncomeForEIC_ACTC` | Earned-income election; all-or-none per spouse | `false`/absent → nontaxable amounts omitted from line 1d and no line 8s produced |
| `hasTradeOrBusinessProvidingHomeCare` | Schedule C path flag | `true` → emits `MEDICAID_WAIVER_SCHEDULE_C_*` blocking flag and returns `(null, null)`. Broadened 2026-05-20: fires regardless of whether any amounts are present |
| `qualifiedIndividualsUnder19Count` | §131(c) cap (under 19) | Absent → treated as 0; no cap fires (legacy data tolerance) |
| `qualifiedIndividualsAge19OrOlderCount` | §131(c) cap (19+) | Absent → treated as 0 |
| `programName`, `livesWithCareRecipient` | Informational / display | No computation effect |

### Per-entry inputs (within `medicaidWaiverPayments[]`)

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `payerName` | Display / matching | Empty string; matching falls back to TIN |
| `payerTIN` | EIN for W-2 cross-reference | Matching falls back to payerName only |
| `sourceType` | Determines W-2 resolution branch (W-2 vs 1099/Other) | Treated as non-W-2; no W-2 lookup |
| `qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount` | Nontaxable excluded amount | Entry contributes only its taxable portion |
| `w2Box12CodeIIAmount` | W-2 box 12 code II (informational) | No warning; present without `qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount` → `MEDICAID_WAIVER_CODE_II_MISSING_QUALIFIED_*` blocking flag |
| `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1` | Qualified portion already in W-2 box 1 wages | W-2 source + absent + box1 > 0 → `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED_*` blocking flag; W-2 source + absent + box1 ≤ 0 → auto-set to 0 |
| `taxablePaymentsNotInW2Box1` | Taxable MW payments not in W-2 box 1 | Absent/zero → no taxable component from this entry |

### W-2 cross-reference (double-count prevention)

When `sourceType == "W-2"`, backend calls `findMatchingW2Entry(w2Entries, ssn, payerTIN, payerName)`:

| Condition | Action |
|---|---|
| Match found AND box 1 > 0 AND `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1` not provided | Emit `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED_{LABEL}` (blocking); `missingIncludedAmount = true`; entry's `qualifiedNotInW2` excluded from accumulation |
| Match found AND box 1 ≤ 0 AND `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1` not provided | Auto-set `includedInW2 = 0`; no flag |
| No W-2 match found | `includedInW2` used as provided (may be null → treated as 0) |

---

## Output 2: `schedule1.additionalIncome.otherIncomeMedicaidWaiverPayments` — Schedule 1 Line 8s

### Formula

```
Per person (only when election == true):
  offset = qualifiedIncludedInW2 + qualifiedNotInW2
  line8s_person = −offset
  where:
    qualifiedIncludedInW2 = Σ over entries of min(qualifiedAmount, includedInW2)
    qualifiedNotInW2      = Σ over entries of max(0, qualifiedAmount − includedInW2)

Aggregated:
  Schedule1.line8s = taxpayer.line8s + spouse.line8s   (both already negative; MFS skips spouse)
```

Line 8s is a **negative number** — entered in the preprinted parentheses on Schedule 1. It backs out the total nontaxable Medicaid waiver amounts that were included either on line 1a (via W-2 box 1) or line 1d (via the earned-income election) so they are not taxed.

Only nontaxable (qualified Notice 2014-7) amounts are backed out. Taxable amounts (`taxableNonW2`) remain on line 1d permanently and are NOT reversed.

### Effect on Schedule 1 totals

```
schedule1.line8 = sum of 8a–8z (includes line 8s, which is negative)
schedule1.line9 = sum of lines 1–7 + line 8
schedule1.line10 = line 9 (carried to Form 1040 line 8)
→ Form1040.line8 → Form1040.line9 (total income)
```

Because line 8s is negative, it reduces total income — offsetting the line 1d gross inclusion and preserving net-zero tax effect on the nontaxable portion.

---

## Earned-Income Election Mechanics

The election is **all-or-none per spouse** — the taxpayer cannot elect partial inclusion.

| Election value | Line 1d includes | Line 8s produced |
|---|---|---|
| `true` | `taxableNonW2 + qualifiedNotInW2` | `−(qualifiedIncludedInW2 + qualifiedNotInW2)` |
| `false` / absent | `taxableNonW2` only | null (no Schedule 1 line 8s entry) |

On MFJ, each spouse may make a different choice. The aggregation in `computeMedicaidWaiverPayments()` sums each spouse's independent result.

---

## Cross-entry Duplication Invariant (verified 2026-05-06)

The per-entry W2_OVERAGE flag (`includedInW2 > qualifiedAmount`) enforces a constraint that makes line 8s algebraically equivalent to `−qualifiedTotal`:

```
line 8s = −(Σ includedInW2 + Σ max(0, qualifiedAmount − includedInW2))
        = −Σ qualifiedAmount   (when W2_OVERAGE constraint holds per entry)
        = −qualifiedTotal
```

**Consequence — AGI is fully protected:** if a user duplicates the same real-world payment across two entries, `qualifiedTotal` is inflated AND the line 8s offset is inflated by the same amount. **Net AGI impact is zero.** Residual risk on earned-income basis (EIC, ACTC) when election is ON is tracked as a deferred UI heuristic.

---

## Validation Flags (all blocking)

| Flag | Condition | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_SCHEDULE_C_{LABEL}` | `hasTradeOrBusinessProvidingHomeCare == true` (broadened 2026-05-20 — fires regardless of amounts) | Line 1d path blocked the moment the declaration is made; taxpayer must use Schedule C with Notice 2014-7 expense offset. Returns `(null, null)` |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_BOX1_UNSPECIFIED_{LABEL}` | W-2 source entry; W-2 box 1 > 0; `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1` not provided | `missingIncludedAmount = true`; qualified portion excluded from accumulation |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_CODE_II_MISSING_QUALIFIED_{LABEL}` | `w2Box12CodeIIAmount` present but `qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount` absent | Signals code II identified but Notice 2014-7 qualified amount not entered |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_W2_OVERAGE_{LABEL}` | `qualifiedAmountIncludedInW2Box1 > qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount` | Data entry error |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_ELECTION_NO_QUALIFIED_{LABEL}` | `election == true` but `qualifiedTotal ≤ 0` | Election selected but no qualified amounts to include |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_LINE8S_EXCEEDS_QUALIFIED_{LABEL}` | Computed offset > `qualifiedTotal` | Internal consistency error |
| `MEDICAID_WAIVER_LINE8S_MISSING_{LABEL}` | `election == true` but offset computes to null | Election active but no offset derivable |

`{LABEL}` is either `TAXPAYER` or `SPOUSE`.

---

## Absence / Missing-data Behaviour

| Missing input | Backend behaviour |
|---|---|
| `medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer` form not saved | Person contributes `(null, null)`; no line 1d, no line 8s |
| `receivedMedicaidWaiverPayments == false` or absent | Same as above |
| `medicaidWaiverPayments[]` list empty | Same |
| `election` absent or false | No nontaxable amounts added to line 1d; no line 8s |
| `qualifiedNotice2014_7Amount` absent on entry | Entry contributes only its `taxablePaymentsNotInW2Box1` |
| `taxablePaymentsNotInW2Box1` absent | Treated as zero |
| Both amounts absent for an entry | Entry contributes nothing; no flags |
| Spouse form absent on MFJ | Spouse contributes `(null, null)`; taxpayer stands alone |
| Spouse form present on MFS (stale data) | Backend MFS guard skips `computeMedicaidForPerson(spouse)` entirely — both line 1d and line 8s aggregates exclude spouse |
| §131(c) count fields absent | Treated as 0; no cap fires (legacy data tolerance) |
| Filing status not saved | `isMfs = false` (defaults); spouse form is included if present |

---

## Reference Data

| Constant | Value | Source | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IRC_131C_CAP_INDIVIDUALS_UNDER_19` | 10 | `ReferenceData` (statutory; not tax-year-specific) | §131(c) cap (under-19) |
| `IRC_131C_CAP_INDIVIDUALS_19_OR_OLDER` | 5 | `ReferenceData` | §131(c) cap (19+) |

Line 1d uses NO tax-year-specific monetary constants.

---

## Compute Order

1. **`computeMedicaidWaiverPayments(taxpayerData, spouseData, w2Entries, taxpayerSsn, spouseSsn, isMfs, flags, uid)`** — orchestrator with MFS guard; calls `computeMedicaidForPerson` per spouse and aggregates.
2. **`computeMedicaidForPerson(data, w2Entries, ssn, flags, label, uid)`** — applies outer gates, Schedule C check, per-entry W-2 lookup, §131(c) cap, election logic.
3. **`findMatchingW2Entry(...)`** — SSN + EIN/name match for double-count guard.
4. **`buildIncome()`** — stores `medicaid.line1d` into `income.medicaidWaiverPayments` only when non-null.
5. **`buildSchedule1()`** — reads `medicaid.line8s` into `schedule1.additionalIncome.otherIncomeMedicaidWaiverPayments`; rounded via `roundMoney(...)` only when non-null.

---

## Cross-line Consistency

- **Line 1d feeds `line1z`:** `line1z = 1a + 1b + 1c + 1d + 1e + 1f + 1g + 1h + 1i`. Line 1z feeds line 9 (total income).
- **Line 8s reduces line 8:** Schedule 1 line 8s is one of 8a–8z. Their sum forms line 8, which enters Form 1040 line 8 → line 9.
- **Net effect is zero for nontaxable amounts.** The line 1d gross inclusion for qualified nontaxable amounts is exactly cancelled by the negative line 8s entry. Only lasting impact is on EARNED INCOME — counts for EIC and ACTC even though not taxable.
- **Taxable amounts are permanent.** `taxableNonW2` appears on line 1d with no corresponding line 8s offset.
- **No interaction with Form 4137 or Form 8919.** Medicaid waiver payments are not subject to SS/Medicare under the Notice 2014-7 non-employee path.
- **MFS guard (since 2026-05-06):** Single guard cascades to BOTH outputs via `addNonNull`. UI hides the spouse Medicaid waiver form on non-MFJ as primary defense. Mirrors the line 1c `computeTips` MFS-guard pattern. Test: `line1dMfsGuard_spouseMedicaidWaiverNotAggregatedOnMfs`.
- **W-2 box 12 code II (2025 standard):** Starting with 2024 payments, payers must report nontaxable Medicaid waiver amounts on W-2 box 12 code II (box 1 = 0). The `w2Box12CodeIIAmount` field captures this. Code II is a reporting signal, not a different computation path.

---

## Downstream Consumers

| Consumer | How it uses line 1d |
|---|---|
| Form 1040 line 1z | Direct addNonNull aggregation |
| Form 1040 line 9, AGI | Via line 1z; Schedule 1 line 8 (which includes line 8s) also added → net-zero for nontaxable elected amounts |
| Pub 915 SS Worksheet | Via line 1z; line 8s also reaches the worksheet via Schedule 1 line 8 — net zero preserved |
| Form 2441 earned income | When election=true: nontaxable amounts count toward earned income limit (Notice 2014-7 + IRC §21) |
| EIC earned income (line 27a) | When election=true: included via line 1d |
| Schedule 8812 / ACTC | When election=true: counts toward $2,500 floor |

---

## Deferred / Not Yet Wired

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Schedule C path (sole-prop home-care business) | Blocked via flag; Schedule C itself out of scope |
| Full IRC §131(c) per-individual recomputation beyond pro-rata cap | Out of scope (rare in HCBS contexts) |
| IRA contribution eligibility for excluded MW payments (IRC §219(f)(1) via Notice 2014-7) | Out of scope for line 1d; would be handled by IRA workflow if implemented |
| UI heuristic for cross-entry duplication (earned-income basis residual risk) | Tracked deferred item |
